title: Retime by default when abc -dff
body:
* Calling `abc -dff` now implies the addition of `-D 1` (delay target of 1ps) if `-D` argument is not specified -- without this, ABC's `retime` command will not get called
This is particularly useful for `synth_* -retime` calls in which you can't specify `-D` easily
* `synth_xilinx` to now perform `techmap +/xilinx/ff_map.v` after `abc -dff` call

EDIT:
* Also fixed a nasty S0 -> S1 typo when storing initial flop states!!

On this circuit:
```
module top(input clk, input [7:0] a, output z);
reg [3:0] ff = 4'b0;
always @(posedge clk)
    ff <= {ff[2:0], ^a};
assign z = ff[3];
endmodule
```

Without these two fixes this is what we get: [before.pdf](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/3049763/before.pdf)
After: [after.pdf](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/3049764/after.pdf)
